spec_match.rationale: The page now applies cross-origin() and integrity() to a real CSS url() declaration backed by Deno resource endpoints instead of client-side simulated bytes and headers.

interactive.rationale: The controls are wired to real resource probes and CSS declarations, but the required chrome-devtools-mcp click-through pass was unavailable in this session.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent blurb promises CORS and hash verification behavior, and the demo now uses actual resource endpoints to show those paths.

edge_cases.rationale: The lab covers missing CORS headers, wildcard-vs-credentialed CORS, wrong integrity strings, tampered resource bytes, and unsupported parser fallback.

references.rationale: Includes a direct link to the target ChromeStatus entry and the CSS Values 5 request URL modifiers section.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared CSS variables for local styling, sets button foregrounds when overriding backgrounds, and avoids static code-card behavior.

openQuestions.title: Run the required chrome-devtools browser pass
openQuestions.detail: chrome-devtools-mcp was not exposed in this Codex session, so the repaired controls and native CSS resource requests still need a real Chrome 150/151 or Canary pass. Exercise anonymous, use-credentials, blocked CORS, wrong integrity, and tampered bytes while checking console and network/timing output.

summary: The concept was rebuilt to use live Deno SVG and metadata routes. Static and server checks can verify the route contracts, but the required chrome-devtools-mcp browser evidence could not be captured in this session.
